name: Spatial offset between weak-lensing mass and X-ray gas in the Bullet Cluster
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the observed spatial separation between the weak-lensing mass distribution and the X-ray-emitting baryonic gas in the merging cluster 1E 0657-558 (Bullet Cluster) and similar systems (e.g. MACS J0025.4-1222, the 'Train Wreck' cluster Abell 520). The lensing peaks coincide with the collisionless galaxy distribution and are spatially offset by ~0.7 Mpc from the X-ray gas peaks.

regime.description: Galaxy-cluster-scale gravitational lensing and X-ray emission, post-merger system at z ~ 0.3.

current_bound.reference: Clowe et al., 'A direct empirical proof of the existence of dark matter', ApJ 648, L109 (2006), arXiv:astro-ph/0608407

what_it_excludes: Pure modified-gravity-without-dark-matter proposals (MOND in its original form, TeVeS) cannot accommodate this offset: in those frameworks, gravitational lensing tracks visible mass, but here the lensing mass is nowhere near the dominant baryonic component (the gas). This observation is the cleanest single argument that a non-baryonic, weakly-interacting matter component must exist in some form. Modified-gravity frameworks can only survive by reintroducing a dark-sector matter component (i.e. accepting some form of dark matter), which defeats the original motivation.

parameterization.name: For modified-gravity frameworks: ratio of integrated convergence in the lensing reconstruction to the integrated baryonic surface density along the same line of sight. For dark-matter frameworks: the dark-matter / baryon fraction in the cluster and the relative collisionality of the two components.
parameterization.reference: Clowe et al. 2006 (lensing reconstruction); Markevitch et al. 2004, ApJ 606, 819 (X-ray analysis); Springel and Farrar 2007, MNRAS 380, 911 (N-body simulation of merger).
parameterization.summary: The Bullet Cluster offset constrains the relative drag/collisionality of the gravitating component and the gas. Standard collisionless cold dark matter passes trivially; any framework where gravitating mass tracks gas (pure modified gravity without DM) cannot.

procedural.predicted_observable: Position of the inferred gravitational potential peak vs position of the X-ray gas peak in a cluster merger.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Offset must reproduce the ~0.7 Mpc separation observed; equivalently, lensing convergence must NOT track gas surface density.
framework_inputs: Predicted relation between lensing convergence and matter / baryon distribution in the framework.
If the framework includes a dark-matter-like component, its self-interaction cross section sigma/m must be < ~1 cm^2/g to avoid disrupting cluster mergers.
procedural.notes: Self-interacting dark matter is constrained by similar cluster-merger systems to sigma/m < 0.5-1 cm^2/g. Frameworks proposing strongly-interacting dark sectors must respect this bound.

primary_references.citation: Clowe et al., 'A direct empirical proof of the existence of dark matter', ApJ 648, L109 (2006)
Markevitch et al., 'Direct constraints on the dark matter self-interaction cross-section from the merging galaxy cluster 1E 0657-56', ApJ 606, 819 (2004)
Bradac et al., 'Revealing the properties of dark matter in the merging cluster MACS J0025.4-1222', ApJ 687, 959 (2008)
